metrics.narrative: Business problem confirmed: International SOS runs decentralized procurement across 90 countries with disparate contract repositories (Smartsheet, OneTrust, ServiceNow) causing inefficiency, low transparency, and compliance risk. Jan Frater stated: 'every business line keeping a separate contract repository, inefficiently, taking lots of transparency away, requires double work to pull data back into the procurement repository—an annoying point I'm trying to eliminate' (discovery call 20 May 2026). Pain is operational (manual work, repository fragmentation) and compliance-driven ('more pressure on cost control, compliance, and transparency'). No quantified savings, headcount reduction, or cycle-time improvement articulated yet. Jan mentioned 'cost control' and 'not staffing things to death' but did not quantify ROI. Phase 1 scope: 200-300 IT suppliers, contract and supplier management consolidation, AI-driven intake and workflows. The business case Jan is building for CFO will require quantified value; current state is qualitative pain only.

Jan has direct access to CFO (Claire Chow) and owns the business case build. He is actively driving the process, engaged, and responsive (email 9 Jun 2026, 22 days ago). competition.narrative: OneTrust is the incumbent for supplier management (InfoSec-owned), and Coupa was briefly evaluated and abandoned by the med supplies division. Jan stated: 'OneTrust holds outdated data, lacks unified contract visibility, InfoSec doesn't know which contracts are active or which suppliers are still active—lots of data garbage' and 'Coupa was too tailored for med supplies and quickly abandoned' (discovery call 20 May 2026). OneTrust is the primary threat: it is embedded with InfoSec, and Jan's budget strategy depends on OneTrust replacement or integration synergy to offset Zycus cost ('piggyback on InfoSec finances, enforcing them to use Zycus potentially'). If InfoSec defends OneTrust, synergy case collapses and deal shrinks. Coupa is eliminated (declined). Jan also referenced 'nifty little AI companies' (unnamed AI point-solution vendors) as alternatives but said they 'focus on intake or partial needs, not wrapped into a holistic solution, not sure how mature'. Jan positioned Zycus as 'traditional s two p but closer to the AI side'. Competitive wedge: Zycus delivers full S2C depth (contract, supplier, sourcing, AI intake) vs OneTrust's supplier-only scope and data quality issues, and vs AI point solutions' immaturity. How we win: demonstrate unified contract/supplier/AI platform with OneTrust replacement or integration, validate InfoSec synergy on demo call, and deliver indicative pricing that fits Jan's CFO business case envelope.

identify_pain.narrative: Jan Frater owns the pain and articulated it clearly on discovery call 20 May 2026. Jan stated: 'every business line keeping a separate contract repository, inefficiently, taking lots of transparency away, requires double work to pull data back into the procurement repository—an annoying point I'm trying to eliminate.' OneTrust pain: 'lots of data garbage, InfoSec doesn't know which contracts are active, which suppliers are still active, which went into contract, which are still existing for audit.' Sourcing pain: 'traditional eSourcing requires expert guidance; self-service needed for business lines.' Jan's pain is confirmed, owned, and driving the evaluation. Pain severity: high enough to justify CFO business case build and system overhaul.

decision_criteria.narrative: Jan articulated detailed decision criteria on the discovery call 20 May 2026. Must-haves: (1) unified contract and supplier management replacing disparate repositories (OneTrust, Smartsheet, ServiceNow); (2) AI-driven intake with dynamic field display per business line and supplier context ('self-service guidance layered over the traditional system for different roles'); (3) AI-driven contract data extraction, approval workflow automation, and supplier onboarding with prequalification; (4) flexibility for localized/dynamic configurations per business line ('digital team wants their own information attached, extract it again, but still part of the same thing'); (5) conversational AI for policy queries and template prep (phase 2). Integration: must integrate with the relevant stakeholder without custom APIs. Sourcing is secondary but expected (self-service for tactical RFPs, AI-assisted for strategic). Jan explicitly ruled out 'AI for the sake of AI'; automation must solve real operational pain. Evaluation process: 'beauty contest, not a formal RFP, looking at a few options, I know what I don't want'. Jan will demo Zycus, evaluate 1-2 alternatives, then propose to CFO with business case.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Strong AI positioning opportunity. Products in scope include Merlin Intake (AI-driven intake and workflows). Jan explicitly researched Zycus for AI capabilities and said 'Zycus came out as being well, while still on the traditional s two p side, a little closer at least to the AI side in terms of AI support and enablement' (discovery call 20 May 2026). Jan's must-haves center on AI: dynamic intake with self-service guidance, AI contract data extraction, approval workflow automation, supplier onboarding prequalification, and conversational AI for policy queries (phase 2). Jan ruled out 'AI for the sake of AI' but expects automation to solve real operational pain. Demo agenda (committed 20 May 2026) will highlight AI-enabled workflows and Merlin capabilities. Position Merlin as the automation layer that unifies contract/supplier/sourcing with AI-driven compliance, transparency, and self-service—vs OneTrust's manual data quality issues and AI point solutions' immaturity.

items.quote: Every business line keeping a separate contract repository, inefficiently, taking lots of transparency away, requires double work to pull data back into the procurement repository—an annoying point I'm trying to eliminate.
items.criterion: Unified contract and supplier management replacing disparate repositories (OneTrust, Smartsheet, ServiceNow)

items.quote: Self-service guidance layered over the traditional system for different roles. The digital team wants their own information attached to it, extract it again, but still part of the same thing. System should offer those fields or work with those fields only when relevant.
items.criterion: AI-driven intake with dynamic field display per business line and supplier context (self-service guidance layered over traditional system for different roles)

items.quote: Working with AI around simplifying things, extracting information, contract approval workflow, intake at supplier onboarding. Automate supplier onboarding intake and prequalification with AI checks.
items.criterion: AI-driven contract data extraction, approval workflow automation, supplier onboarding with prequalification

items.quote: I see the traditional SAP will more likely integrate with Infor than a random AI grown company where the ERP implementation might be lagging behind.
items.criterion: Integration with Infor ERP without custom APIs

items.quote: I could piggyback on the finances, enforcing InfoSec to use Zycus potentially. It might be good to get one of those InfoSec guys on the call to have a look and see if there's an opportunity for some synergy there.
items.criterion: OneTrust replacement or integration to consolidate supplier management and enable budget synergy

items.quote: Conversational AI on top which gives information on policies, helps to prepare templates, maybe helps in negotiations, but that's definitely not the phase one focus.
items.criterion: Conversational AI for policy queries, template preparation, negotiation support (phase 2)

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong fit. Jan's must-haves align with Zycus core capabilities: unified contract and supplier management (iContract, iSupplier), AI-driven intake with dynamic field display (Merlin Intake), AI contract data extraction and approval workflows (iContract + Merlin), supplier onboarding with prequalification (iSupplier), conversational AI (Merlin), and Infor ERP integration. Demo agenda (committed 20 May 2026) will highlight contract lifecycle, supplier workflows, AI intelligence, and self-service guidance. Jan's phase 1 scope (200-300 IT suppliers, contract/supplier management) maps to Zycus iContract + iSupplier + Merlin Intake. Sourcing (iSource) is secondary but expected. Jan explicitly researched Zycus and said 'Zycus came out as being well, while still on the traditional s two p side, a little closer at least to the AI side'. One exposure: OneTrust replacement/integration synergy must be validated with InfoSec stakeholders on demo call to derisk Jan's budget strategy.
